name: Brunswick Stew with Corn Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 1/4 tsp dried thyme or 4 sprigs fresh thyme
6 lb stewing chicken (or large roaster, quail)
1 cup dried baby lima beans (soaked overnight)
recipeInstructions:
Soak lima beans overnight in cold water then drain.
Combine the peppercorns, cloves, garlic, bay leaves, parsley, thyme, and bacon with a string in cheesecloth. Add to an 8-quart pot with the stock and bring to a simmer over medium-high heat. Add chicken; simmer, covered, until tender (about 1 1/2 to 2 hours). Chill overnight if desired.
Skim fat from surface of stock. Remove chicken; discard skin and bones and cut meat into bite-size pieces. Set aside.
Cook bacon in a medium skillet over medium heat until crisp. Remove with slotted spoon to paper towel and drain well. Add to stock.
Pour off all but 2 tablespoons fat, then add remaining onion and sauté quickly until browned. Add to stock along with potatoes, tomatoes, lima beans, tomato paste, red pepper, salt, and pepper, stirring to combine.
Cover partially and simmer until beans are tender (about 40-60 minutes). Stew can be prepared several days ahead to this point and refrigerated.
Stir in reserved chicken, okra, and corn. Bring stew to a simmer, then drop dumpling batter by tablespoonfuls. Cover pot securely and simmer until dumplings are puffed and a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean (about 15 minutes).
Turn into soup tureen to serve or spoon dumplings into heated bowls and ladle soup over.
Corn Dumplings:
Combine flour, cornmeal, baking powder, salt, and sugar in a medium bowl. Mix well.
Cut butter in until mixture resembles coarse meal. Stir in corn using fork, then add milk and stir just until moistened; do not overmix. Add to stew and cook as directed.
